decision: Push channel = Discord webhook (single HTTP POST, JSON {content}). Implement Discord first.
reasoning: PROJECT_SPEC V2 requires choosing one push channel first. Discord webhook is the simplest transport — one secret (URL), no auth handshake, renders Markdown near-directly. Aligns with lean 'smallest valuable thing first'.
rejected: Slack webhook (needs Markdown->mrkdwn conversion)
Email SMTP (host/port/user/pass/TLS/MIME — most moving parts)

decision: Push is a THIRD output_destination value ('discord'), mutually exclusive with file/stdout (one destination per run).
reasoning: Preserves the existing 'deliver ONCE' contract (BR-7-007) and single-value output_destination schema. Adds one enum value + one adapter + one elif branch. Fits tiny scope.
rejected: Parallel delivery (file + discord) — breaks deliver-once, needs multi-delivery orchestration
destinations list — breaks single-value schema, large config+pipeline refactor

decision: Discord push failure (4xx/5xx/timeout/network) is FATAL: raise DeliveryError -> 'Error:' on stderr -> exit 1. Explicit request timeout (~10s) to avoid hanging the pipeline.
reasoning: Consistent with existing file-delivery contract (fatal DeliveryError). Simple, no retry logic. User sees a clear error and re-runs. Retry/backoff deferred to V3.
rejected: Non-fatal warn+exit0 — silently loses digest if Discord down
Fatal+retry-with-backoff — adds retry loop + 429 Retry-After handling, more moving parts

decision: Digest exceeding Discord's 2000-char content limit is split into multiple sequential messages at repo-section boundaries.
reasoning: Preserves full digest content (product goal: read digest, understand repo). Plain content transport, no embed schema. Splitting on natural '## repo' boundaries is clean and testable.
rejected: Truncate at 2000 — loses content, defeats product goal
Discord embeds (4096/6000 limits) — needs embed JSON build, still must split beyond 6000

decision: Webhook URL is read from an env var (default DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL, name configurable in [output]); a single repo section still >2000 chars is hard-split by line at 2000.
reasoning: Env-var secret matches security.md + existing github_token/llm_api_key pattern. Hard-split-by-line fallback guarantees every message is <=2000 (repo-split first, line-split if still too long) — no message ever exceeds the limit.
rejected: URL in config file — violates security.md (secret must not be committed)
Fatal error on oversized section — brittle for large repos

decision: DiscordDelivery must not import osspulse.github/summarizer/cache/render — verified by static import inspection.
reasoning: Extends the living-spec constraint AC-6-002 to the new adapter; keeps delivery decoupled. Signed off at SPEC LOCK.

decision: A single repo section >2000 chars is hard-split by line so no message exceeds 2000.
reasoning: CLAR-5 fallback — repo-boundary split alone can't guarantee <=2000 for a pathological section. Signed off.
rejected: fatal error on oversized section

decision: The 2000 limit is counted in Unicode characters (code points), not UTF-8 bytes.
reasoning: Matches Discord's own counting; a byte-based split would over-split non-ASCII digests. Signed off.
rejected: byte-length counting

decision: The webhook URL never appears in any error message, log, or run-summary.
reasoning: STRIDE T1 — URL is a bearer secret. Signed off at SPEC LOCK.

decision: destination=discord with unset/empty webhook env var raises ConfigError at load.
reasoning: CLAR-5 — env var is required; fail fast before the pipeline runs. Signed off.
rejected: defer to delivery time

decision: A non-https webhook URL raises ConfigError.
reasoning: STRIDE T3 — https enforced for TLS integrity + SSRF guard. Signed off.

decision: A webhook host not in {discord.com,discordapp.com} raises ConfigError.
reasoning: STRIDE T2/T3 — host allowlist blocks mis-route + SSRF to internal hosts. Signed off at SPEC LOCK.
rejected: accept any host

decision: Reuse httpx (already httpx>=0.27) for the Discord POST; no new dependency.
reasoning: httpx is already first-class (github/client.py uses httpx.Client(timeout=...)); zero added supply-chain surface, reuses a known timeout pattern.
rejected: add requests
stdlib urllib.request

decision: Character-based two-level split: greedy-accumulate ## repo sections up to 2000 chars, hard-split oversized sections by line (defensively char-slice a >2000 line). Measured with len(str).
reasoning: Preserves readable repo blocks, guarantees <=2000, matches renderer ## boundaries, and counts Unicode code points not bytes (AC-V2-005-006/007).
rejected: blind fixed-size char chunks (cuts mid-line)
byte-based split (over-splits non-ASCII)

decision: Resolve webhook URL from env var + validate https scheme + Discord host allowlist in load_config; ConfigError on failure. Config gains webhook_url + webhook_env fields.
reasoning: Fail-fast before any network (T2/T3 SSRF guard); secret never in config file (T1); mirrors existing _resolve_token env pattern. DiscordDelivery receives the pre-validated URL.
rejected: lazy validation inside DiscordDelivery at deliver time (fails late, scatters security check)

decision: No openapi.yaml — OSS Pulse is a CLI tool with no HTTP API (outbound client only).
reasoning: Integration seam is a Python Protocol + config + CLI wiring (INT-V2-005-001), not an exposed endpoint. Rule R5/R9 cited. Precedent: delivery-6 ADR-007, digest-renderer-5 ADR-005, state-store-3 ADR-004.
rejected: write an openapi.yaml for a tool with no API

decision: Added webhook_url: str | None = None and webhook_env: str = 'DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL' as last two fields on Config dataclass with defaults so existing construction sites are unaffected.
reasoning: Frozen dataclass — defaults required to avoid breaking callers that don't pass new fields. None signals 'not discord mode'.
rejected: required fields (would break all existing Config() calls)

decision: Added _resolve_discord_url(output_section, env) helper returning (url, env_name); called only when destination=='discord'. Error messages name the env var and constraint but never the URL value.
reasoning: Mirrors _resolve_token / _resolve_llm pattern exactly. Separation into a named helper keeps load_config readable and the helper unit-testable in isolation.
rejected: inline validation inside load_config (harder to test independently)

decision: _split_for_discord scans the raw string char-by-char to find ## repo boundaries (look for '## ' preceded by newline), then greedy-accumulates sections. Extracted _split_lines and _char_slice as helpers + _enforce_limit safety-net. All pure functions for easy testing.
reasoning: Keeps split logic composable and each helper independently testable. Safety-net _enforce_limit guards against future edge cases without changing the main algorithm.
rejected: single monolithic split function (harder to unit-test individual levels)

decision: DiscordDelivery uses an injected httpx.Client (client param) for tests; production uses a context-managed httpx.Client(timeout=self._timeout) created inside deliver(). TimeoutException caught before RequestError (subclass ordering). Error messages use type(exc).__name__ for network errors, response.status_code for HTTP errors — no URL or str(exc) involved.
reasoning: TimeoutException is a subclass of RequestError in httpx — must catch it first to emit the correct 'timed out' message. str(exc) for httpx errors would embed the URL in some cases.
rejected: catch RequestError only (TimeoutException would fall into wrong branch)
use str(exc) in error message (leaks URL)

decision: test_port_compatibility uses duck-type inspection (callable(getattr(d,'deliver',None))) instead of isinstance(d, Delivery).
reasoning: Delivery Protocol is not decorated with @runtime_checkable — isinstance() raises TypeError at runtime. Duck-type check verifies the same structural property.
rejected: isinstance check (TypeError at runtime)
add @runtime_checkable to ports.py (out of scope, modifies shared file)

decision: GO — 0 Critical/High bugs, 15/15 ACs verified, 103/103 tests pass, coverage 96%, security audit 0 Critical/High, all tasks [x].
reasoning: Independent test run matches dev claim. SSRF guard, URL-leak guard, https enforcement, timeout, import decoupling all verified via code review + smoke tests. Two SHALLOW_TC gaps (SH-001/002) are non-blocking. Pipeline discord branch (lines 291-294) uncovered but trivial wiring — delivery tested independently.
rejected: NO-GO (no blockers found)
